A_protein_only.evaluation_rigor: HIGH — proper nested CV avoids optimistic bias

B_methylation_only.evaluation_rigor: LOW — n=24, LOO-CV, p>>n (20 features from 298 candidates on 24 samples), high overfitting risk

C_combined.method: Protein (n=121) + methylation score injected as single feature
C_combined.evaluation_rigor: MEDIUM — protein CV on n=121, methylation score from n=24 LOO-CV injected

operating_point_paradox.observation: Combined AUC (0.900) > protein AUC (0.866) but combined sens@95spec (62.7%) < protein sens@95spec (67.8%)
operating_point_paradox.explanation: AUC measures average performance across ALL operating points. Adding methylation improves overall discrimination (AUC +0.034). However, at the 95% specificity operating point specifically, the combined model's decision threshold shifts. The methylation score, derived from a tiny n=24 cohort, adds noise at this extreme operating point. The protein-only model's calibration at 95% specificity happens to be more stable because it was trained on n=121 samples. This is a known phenomenon: AUC improvement does not guarantee improvement at every operating point, especially at high-specificity thresholds where small calibration shifts can flip individual classifications.
operating_point_paradox.clinical_implication: For a screening test requiring 95% specificity, the combined model is WORSE despite higher AUC. The methylation score needs a larger training cohort to be useful at this operating point.

issues: Sample size n=24 (12 CRC, 12 control) is critically small
Feature selection: 20 features chosen from 298 candidates on 24 samples (ratio 12.4:1 features:samples) — severe overfitting risk
LOO-CV leaves only 1 sample out at a time — high variance estimator with n=24
Sensitivity CI: [58.3%, 100.0%] based on only 10/12 correct detections
Feature selection performed INSIDE or OUTSIDE the LOO loop is critical — if outside, results are invalid due to data leakage
AUC=0.979 with 20 features on 24 samples is a classic overfitting signature

comparison_to_cmcd.verdict: Our methylation sensitivity (83.3%) is suspiciously close to the cMCD benchmark (87.8%) but on 26x fewer samples. This is more likely overfitting than true equivalence.

what_would_validate_it: Independent test set of >=50 CRC + 50 control cfDNA methylation samples
Feature selection strictly inside the CV loop (verify with developer)

assumptions.targeted_bisulfite_panel: $50-100 for a targeted panel of 20 CpGs (not per-CpG)
assumptions.note: Targeted bisulfite panels cost per-panel, not per-CpG, unlike the $75/marker assumed in LASSO optimization

cost_per_correct_diagnosis.note: At CRC prevalence 4.1% in screening-age adults

recommendation.optimal_architecture: B — Methylation-only, IF validated on larger cohort
recommendation.rationale: Methylation-only shows the highest sensitivity (83.3% at 95% spec) and lowest per-test cost ($50-100). However, this is based on n=24 LOO-CV and is almost certainly inflated. The protein-only panel is the only architecture with reliable validation (nested CV, n=121) but achieves only 47.7% sensitivity at 95% spec — below the 70% project target. The combined approach paradoxically HURTS at the 95% operating point.
recommended_path: 1. IMMEDIATE: Validate methylation classifier with developer — confirm feature selection is inside LOO loop
2. SHORT-TERM: Request developer build bootstrap validation for methylation classifier (1000 resamples)
3. MEDIUM-TERM: Acquire independent cfDNA methylation validation cohort (n>=100)
4. ARCHITECTURE: Pursue methylation-primary panel with protein-backup for methylation-negative/borderline cases
5. COST: Targeted bisulfite panel ($50-100) is more cost-effective than protein panel ($105) IF sensitivity holds

rd_flag.caveat: Based on n=24 LOO-CV — validation required before escalating to research director
rd_flag.action: Create RD task noting the >80% finding with prominent caveat about sample size

lasso_cost_weight_issue.finding: LASSO optimization uses $75 PER methylation marker, but targeted bisulfite costs $50-100 per PANEL
lasso_cost_weight_issue.impact: Methylation markers are penalized ~15x too heavily in the cost-weighted LASSO (75/marker vs ~5/marker for a 20-CpG panel)
lasso_cost_weight_issue.evidence: At C=0.127 (optimal), 0 methylation markers selected. At C=0.234, 11 methylation markers enter but AUC=1.0 (overfit on n=24)
lasso_cost_weight_issue.recommendation: Developer should re-run LASSO with corrected methylation cost: $5-7 per CpG (=$100 panel / ~15-20 CpGs) instead of $75/CpG
